posts.text: You are so inspiring <3 #fairycreek 11,000+ people went to send letters to protect old growth forests on Vancouver Island in about a week! Now, we wait for the government’s decision. You can call the Premier and let him know your thoughts at 250-387-1715; just know it will be an international call if you live outside Canada so you may have to pay depending on where you live. Feel free to use the letter writing instructions at the link in my bio to help frame what you say when you call, and if yo

posts.text: Vibes of the Canadian PNW 🌧️ Some of the biggest trees on Earth hide here on Vancouver Island, so over 4,000 people have gone to send letters to protect its old growth rainforests from being cut down. The individual trees can be over 1,000 years old, but antique yellow cedar forests in their entirety are a special class of forest dating back to the last Ice Age that can be over 11,000 years old. They’re very rare, breathtakingly beautiful and hold the precious kind of deep, complex biodiversity

posts.text: Wait for the soft drop… We have 3 days to save old growth forests like this in British Columbia, Canada.  Solène and others with the Fairy Creek Blockade attempted to prevent this clear cut from happening before being arr3sted by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police for protecting the land. 57% of the softwood lumber exported from British Columbia goes to the US. Laws like the Endangered Species Act down here in the States make it harder to do such intense logging in public land down here; there ar

posts.text: POV: you visit the oldest forests in Canada, which we can save from clear cutting by sending a many letters as we can at the link in my bio. I know it can feel overwhelming, but you aren’t in this alone. Figured it might help others to all have a moment where we talk about things in the forest together. 57% of the softwood lumber from this province of Canada gets exported to the USA, and the province just opened a public comment period open to international participants. posts.text: Reflections from the rainforest - we have until 2/22/2026 to submit public comments to save this place. Link will be in my bio until then 🫡 See my other vids for more context! All love to the incredible people of Canada, hoping this brings our countries together ❤️ Thank you to Pacheedaht Elder Bill Jones and those at Fairy Creek for welcoming me so warmly, and @aidinrobbins for snagging the footage #environmentalscience #outdoors

posts.text: We have 5 days to save this forest - in both the US & Canada, road paths on public land get aimed through big trees as part of logging projects for different reasons. In the U.S., it’s because the trees are subject to weaker protections as part of a “road building” project than they are as part of a logging project. In Canada, it’s because trees cut down as part of “road building” don’t have to be paid for. Slightly different reasons with the same result. The forest in this video is in the tradi

posts.text: What can we learn from Canada? We have until 2/22/2026 to submit public comments; link will be in my bio until then 🫡 All love to the incredible people of Canada, hoping this brings our countries together ❤️ Thank you to Pacheedaht Elder Bill Jones and those at Fairy Creek for welcoming me so warmly, and @Aidin Robbins and Joshua Wright for the footage.
